desc: Round-5 C2: an interpolation containing an inline arrow (illegal in Angular templates) is hoisted into a generated `protected get interp_N()` class-body getter (hoistNonPureTemplateExpression). A TS `as`-cast inside that arrow was wrongly rewritten to Angular's template-only `$any(...)` call — which is undefined in a TS class-body, producing a broken getter (free identifier `$any`). Fix (rewriteTemplateExpression.ts hoistNonPureTemplateExpression): the lowering opts passed to the hoisted getter body set `prefixThis: true` but omitted `scriptContext: true`. `scriptContext` is what tells the rewriter it is emitting real TS (where `as`/`!` must stay intact) vs. an Angular template string (where `X as T` lowers to `$any(X)`). Since the getter body is real TS class-body code, it must pass `scriptContext: true` alongside `prefixThis: true`, so the emitted getter becomes `return this.items().filter(x => (x as number) > 1).length;` instead of the broken `$any(x)` form. The other five targets allow inline arrows directly in the template/JSX expression (no hoist needed), so they were already correct. Found by the shape-matrix harness (as-cast-cross/as-nested-arrow-interp).
